meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank screen. Learn to use analytics to discover real-world user needs, turning data into your first high-impact AI project with Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' paralysis ends when you stop guessing and start measuring. By integrating lightweight analytics into a simple MVP, you identify real user friction points. Use Antigravity to scaffold the tracking and Claude Code to implement it, transforming raw user behavior into your next breakthrough feature.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in the void—a rhythmic, mocking reminder that your portfolio remains empty. You’ve mastered the syntax, but you lack the map to build something that actually matters. Most students fall into the trap of cloning to-do apps, but the real innovators build solutions to problems they can prove exist.

Relief isn't found in a brainstorming list; it's found in the data. By deploying a minimal analytics layer, you stop building for yourself and start building for the market. This guide transforms you from a passive coder into a data-driven AI builder, using professional-grade tools to turn user behavior into your project roadmap.

steps.title: Define Your Data Hypothesis
steps.description: Identify what 'success' looks like for your app. Before writing code, determine which user interaction reveals the biggest pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Create a tracking schema for a web app that captures user drop-off points during an AI-driven workflow. Define 3 key events that prove user value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a schema.md file documenting the 3 key event tracking points identified by the product engineer prompt'

steps.title: Scaffold the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate a privacy-first analytics provider. We need a clean, non-intrusive way to pipe event data to a dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular analytics provider class in TypeScript that can handle event batching and error logging for a Next.js application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the analytics provider class into /lib/analytics.ts and configure environment variables'

steps.title: Instrument the Critical Path
steps.description: Attach tracking hooks to your UI components. Focus on where the user interacts with your AI model's output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom React hook 'useTrackEvent' that wraps analytics calls and ensures we capture user session IDs and event metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor the main chat component to use the useTrackEvent hook on button clicks and AI response completion'

steps.title: Build the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Analytics are useless without insight. Connect your event logs to a simple dashboard to visualize where users struggle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to aggregate analytics JSON logs from the last 24 hours and output a summary of the most frequent user navigation paths.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a script /scripts/analyze-usage.ts that parses local logs and prints a summary to the console'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Evidence
steps.description: Now that you have data, pivot your project features to address the friction points discovered in Step 4.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on a high drop-off rate at the AI generation step, suggest 3 UI/UX improvements to keep users engaged and reduce latency anxiety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the suggested UI improvements to the chat interface to reduce user friction'

pro_tips: Always use batching for analytics events to avoid blocking the main thread and slowing down your UI.
Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information); use hashed session IDs to keep user data private and compliant.
Set up 'heartbeat' events to measure how long a user stays on a page, which is a stronger indicator of value than a simple click.
Use feature flags to toggle analytics; it allows you to test different tracking granularities without redeploying your entire codebase.
Treat your logs as a 'product backlog'—the most frequent errors or exit points are your next feature development opportunities.
resources: Analytics Best Practices for SaaS: https://posthog.com/blog
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, deploy your app to a public URL, share it with 5 friends, and monitor the dashboard for 48 hours to validate your first feature hypothesis.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Use data to validate your impact, refine your skills, and prove your worth to future employers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
